steps.title: Identify the mismatch
steps.content: The passage describes readers who “try to identify more and more real-life analogues” in a novel that “is largely pure invention.” This contradiction—searching for real-world basis in mostly fictional material—creates the risk.

steps.title: Apply the logic to the choices
steps.content:
When you search for real-world connections in a mostly fictional work, you propose links without actual factual support—the unsupportable connections the correct answer describes.

The other choices either reverse what the passage says readers are doing, introduce unrelated topics, or miss the problem entirely.

A is correct.
